q: Which of the following is part of the Basis and Purpose of the Amateur Radio Service?

choices.A: Providing personal radio communications for as many citizens as possible
choices.C: Advancing skills in the technical and communication phases of the radio art

choices.A: It is required when transmitting emergency messages
choices.B: It is encouraged when using phone emissions
choices.C: It is required when in contact with foreign stations

choices.C: An amateur station located more than 50 km above Earth's surface

choices.B: Membership in the Amateur Radio Emergency Service (ARES)

q: Which of the following is prohibited?

choices.A: International communications on VHF bands and higher frequencies

What is a common repeater frequency offset in the 70-centimeter band?
What is an appropriate way to call another station on a repeater if you know the other station's call sign?

choices.A: Say "break, break," then the other station’s call sign, followed by your call sign
choices.B: Say the station's call sign, then identify with your call sign
choices.C: Say "CQ" three times, then the other station's call sign, followed by your call sign
choices.D: Wait for the station to call CQ, then answer with your call sign

q: How should you respond to a station calling CQ?

choices.A: Transmit "CQ" followed by the other station’s call sign
choices.B: Transmit your call sign followed by the other station’s call sign
choices.C: Transmit the other station’s call sign followed by your call sign
choices.D: Transmit a signal report followed by your call sign

q: What is an effective way to seek a call from any phone station when not using a repeater?

choices.A: Sign your call sign once, followed by the words “listening for a call;” if there is no answer, change the frequency and repeat
choices.B: Say “QTC,” followed by “this is” and your call sign; if there is no answer, change the frequency and repeat
choices.C: Transmit an unmodulated carrier for approximately 10 seconds, followed by “this is” and your call sign, and pause to listen; repeat as necessary
choices.D: Repeat “CQ” a few times, followed by “this is,” and your call sign, then pause to listen; repeat as necessary

q: What does the term "repeater offset" mean?

choices.A: The difference between a repeater’s transmit and receive frequencies
choices.B: The repeater has a time delay to prevent interference
choices.C: The approximately half-second delay to allow for the “squelch tail” to be removed

q: What is the meaning of the procedural signal “CQ”?

choices.A: A shortened form of the term “Contest QSO”
choices.B: “Communication Quality,” used to indicate transmitted audio clarity
choices.C: Only the called station should transmit

q: Which of the following is a customary way to indicate a station is listening on a repeater and looking for a contact?

choices.A: “CQ CQ” followed by the repeater’s call sign
choices.B: The station’s call sign followed by the word “listening”
choices.C: The repeater’s call sign followed by the station’s call sign

choices.C: A list of available net frequencies
choices.D: A plan devised by a club to indicate frequency band usage

q: What term describes an amateur station that is transmitting and receiving on the same frequency?

choices.C: To listen on a repeater’s input frequency
choices.D: To listen on a repeater’s output frequency

choices.A: The stations should negotiate continued use of the frequency

choices.A: So stations within range of each other can communicate without tying up a repeater

q: Which of the following is a “talkgroup”?

choices.A: Wavelength gets longer as frequency increases
choices.B: Wavelength gets shorter as frequency increases
